title: Malaria — Severe Plasmodium falciparum with Cerebral Involvement

patient_data.chief_complaint: High cyclical fever for 5 days with confusion and dark urine
symptoms: Rigors and chills alternating with profuse sweating
patient_data.history: Returned from Sub-Saharan Africa (Nigeria) 10 days ago. Did NOT take malaria prophylaxis. No bed net used.

expected_output.primary_diagnosis: Severe falciparum malaria with cerebral involvement
should_detect: SEVERE malaria criteria: parasitemia >5%, cerebral involvement (GCS<15), severe anemia, AKI, hypoglycemia
IV artesunate is FIRST LINE for severe malaria — NOT oral therapy
Urgent thick and thin blood smear for species confirmation
Rapid diagnostic test (RDT) for P. falciparum
Monitor and correct hypoglycemia (quinine can worsen it)
Travel history without prophylaxis — highest risk
